* fix: update E2E nav selectors for module-driven ActivityBar buttons (#252)

* fix: update E2E nav selectors for module-driven ActivityBar buttons

The §29 Phase 3 migration (8a81e9e) changed nav items from <a> links
to <button> elements, but E2E tests still used getByRole('link').
This caused all navigation-related E2E tests to time out at 31.5s.

Changes:
- ActivityBar: add aria-current="page" to active nav button (a11y)
- navigation.ts: update selectors from role=link to role=button
- navigation.ts: add Sessions to NAV_ITEMS, reorder by module sort order
- p6-knowledge, p7-self-improvement, p8-escalation: fix nav selectors

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: resolve strict mode violation in E2E setup wizard Copy button test

The OAuth step now shows two Copy buttons (homepage URL + callback URL).
Use .first() to avoid Playwright strict mode violation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: §29.5.10 settings auditability — log module settings changes to audit log (#251)

Add audit logging for module settings changes per spec §29.5.10:

- Register 4 new audit event types in AuditEventCatalog:
  module.settings.updated, module.settings.reset, module.enabled, module.disabled
- Inject IAuditLog into ModuleController
- Log settings updates (global + project-scoped) with previous/new values
- Log settings resets with cleared key list
- Log module enable/disable actions
- Secret values redacted as '***' in all audit payloads
- Audit logging is best-effort (try/catch) — failures don't fail the request
- Project-scoped updates resolve previous values per-key via GetForProjectAsync

Spec updated: §29.5.10 marked as delivered with implementation details.
Phase 4 checklist updated.

Tests: 9 new tests in ModuleControllerAuditTests covering:
- Settings update audit with previous/new values
- Secret redaction in audit payloads
- Masked secret skip (no audit for unchanged secrets)
- Project-scoped settings audit
- Settings reset audit
- Enable/disable audit
- No audit on validation failure
- Audit failure resilience (request still succeeds)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: §29.5.9 settings provenance tracking — show value source in Settings UI (#254)

Display provenance badges (Default/Global) next to module settings fields
so users can see which layer a resolved value comes from.

- Add Default property to C# ModuleSettingsFieldSchema for manifest defaults
- Compute provenance client-side from stored values vs schema defaults
- Show effective values including defaults when no stored value exists
- Update spec §29.5.9 with implementation details

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: §29.3.9 prefetch hints — low-priority module bundle prefetching (#257)

Add PrefetchManager that accepts manifest-level prefetch hints from modules
declaring which other modules they commonly navigate to. Prefetched bundles
are fetched at low priority via requestIdleCallback (setTimeout fallback)
with concurrency control (max 2 concurrent, max 5 queued).

Changes:
- New: prefetchManager.ts — manages loader registry, queue, budget, cancel
- types.ts: add prefetchModules?: string[] to ModuleManifest + DTO + validation
- coreModules.ts: extract import fns for loader registration; Chat prefetches
  Agents and Sessions
- ModuleContainer.tsx: trigger prefetch when module reaches Ready/Mounted
- moduleRegistry.ts: cancel queued prefetches on disable (both own and hinted)
- 16 new tests covering queue, dedup, cancel, budget, concurrency, failure

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: §29.7.7 per-module performance monitoring (#259)

Add frontend-only metrics collector that tracks per-module lifecycle
events (init timing, mount count, suspend/resume, faults) and surfaces
them in the Settings → Modules UI.

New files:
- moduleMetrics.ts — singleton collector with timer API, ring buffer,
  onChange subscriptions
- ModulePerformancePanel.tsx — diagnostic UI with metric grid and
  collapsible event log
- moduleMetrics.test.ts — 19 unit tests

Instrumentation:
- ModuleContainer: init timing (wraps onInit), mount tracking with
  StrictMode dedup via compound key ref, fault/dispose events
- moduleRegistry: suspend, resume, dispose, render-crash events

Spec §29.7.7 updated from MAY/planned to implemented.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: mask secret fields in GetProjectSettings response (#256) (#260)

The GetProjectSettings endpoint returned decrypted secret values to the
client. Now checks ModuleSettingsFieldSchema.Secret for each key and
returns '***' for secret fields, consistent with GetAllAsync masking.

Added test: GetProjectSettings_MasksSecretFields

Closes #256

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: §29.7.8 memory leak detection in dev mode

Track module memory footprint before init and after dispose + GC delay.
Warn in console and Performance panel if retained memory exceeds 50 MB.

- Uses performance.measureUserAgentSpecificMemory() with legacy fallback
- Versioned snapshots prevent race conditions on rapid remount
- Gated behind import.meta.env.DEV — tree-shaken in production

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add per-project module visibility configuration. Projects can specify

commit.message:
fix: report-incomplete fails pipeline, percent-encode user_id, stage-1 status validation, merge_strategy validation, dead code removal (#141)

* feat: add 8 new safe output tools

Implements the remaining safe output features from GitHub issues #72-#79:

- add-pr-comment (#72): Create comment threads on Azure DevOps pull requests
  with support for general and file-specific inline comments
- link-work-items (#73): Create relationships between work items (parent/child,
  related, predecessor/successor, duplicate)
- queue-build (#74): Trigger Azure DevOps pipeline/build runs with optional
  branch and template parameters
- create-git-tag (#75): Create annotated git tags on commits in ADO repositories
- add-build-tag (#76): Add tags to Azure DevOps builds for classification
- create-branch (#77): Create new branches without immediately creating a PR
- update-pr (#78): Update PR metadata (reviewers, labels, auto-complete, vote,
  description)
- upload-attachment (#79): Upload file attachments to work items

Each tool includes:
- Params struct with validation
- Config struct for front matter configuration
- Executor with full ADO REST API implementation
- Sanitize impl for security
- Comprehensive unit tests

All 8 tools are registered in the MCP server, Stage 2 executor dispatch,
budget system, and write-permissions validation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address PR review feedback

Bug fixes:
- create-git-tag: resolve HEAD via repository's defaultBranch instead of
  hardcoding 'heads/main' (works with master, develop, etc.)
- update-pr: resolve reviewer emails to GUIDs via VSSPS identity API
  before adding to PR (ADO reviewers endpoint requires user ID, not email)

Security:
- update-pr: require 'allowed-votes' to be explicitly configured for vote
  operation — empty list now rejects all votes to prevent accidental
  auto-approve by agents

Improvements:
- update-pr: make auto-complete merge options configurable via front matter
  (delete-source-branch, merge-strategy) instead of hardcoding squash+delete
- add-pr-comment: validate file_path in Validate::validate() at Stage 1
  (was only validated at Stage 2, giving no feedback to agent)
- queue-build: sanitize template parameter keys and values to prevent
  ##vso[] command injection in build logs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: align with gh-aw — add review tools and enhancements

Adds 4 new safe output tools to align with gh-aw's PR review capabilities:

- submit-pr-review: Submit PR review with decision (approve, request-changes,
  comment) and optional body/rationale text. Requires allowed-events config.
- reply-to-pr-review-comment: Reply to existing review comment threads
- resolve-pr-review-thread: Resolve/reactivate review threads (fixed, wont-fix,
  closed, by-design, active)
- report-incomplete: Signal task could not complete due to infrastructure failure

Enhancements to existing tools:
- add-pr-comment: Add start_line param for multi-line code comments (gh-aw parity)
- link-work-items: Set DEFAULT_MAX=5 (matches gh-aw link-sub-issue default)
- queue-build: Set DEFAULT_MAX=3 (prevents pipeline queue flooding)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address second round of PR review feedback

Bug fixes:
- update-pr: derive connectiondata URL from org_url instead of hardcoding
  dev.azure.com — supports vanity domains and national cloud endpoints
- update-pr: derive VSSPS identity URL from org_url instead of hardcoding
  vssps.dev.azure.com — same environment portability fix
- submit-pr-review: same connectiondata URL fix (derived from org_url)

Security:
- upload-attachment: add canonicalize() + prefix check after resolving file
  path to prevent symlink escape attacks (symlinks within workspace could
  previously be followed to read arbitrary files outside source_directory)

Improvements:
- add-pr-comment: sanitize structural fields (repository, status, file_path)
  with control-char stripping for defense-in-depth consistency

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: branch wildcard matching + VSSPS URL warning

- queue-build: drop equality arm from release/* wildcard matching so bare
  'release' no longer matches 'release/*' — only 'release/...' does
- update-pr: add warn! log when VSSPS URL derivation produces no change
  (surfaces the issue for visualstudio.com-hosted orgs at runtime)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: status mapping, VSSPS rejection, branch sanitize, policy order

Bug fix:
- add-pr-comment: fix WontFix status mapped to 6 (should be 3). Unify status
  strings to kebab-case (active, fixed, wont-fix, closed, by-design) with
  CamelCase accepted for backwards compatibility.

Security:
- update-pr: reject add-reviewers for non-dev.azure.com org URLs instead of
  silently proceeding with a malformed VSSPS URL (*.visualstudio.com orgs)

Improvements:
- queue-build: sanitize branch field for defense-in-depth consistency
- update-pr: document allow-list semantics asymmetry (operations=permissive,
  votes=secure) in UpdatePrConfig doc comment
- create-branch: check config policy (allowed-repositories) before resolving
  repo alias, so operators see a policy error not a checkout-list error

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: repo policy ordering, VSSPS loop hoist, dedup resolve_repo_name

Bug fixes:
- create-git-tag: check config allowed-repositories before resolving repo
  alias (same fix as create-branch — policy error before resolution error)
- update-pr: hoist VSSPS URL derivation and validation out of the
  per-reviewer loop (avoids repeated work, fails fast before any API calls)

Refactor:
- Extract resolve_repo_name() to tools/mod.rs — was duplicated identically
  in update_pr.rs and submit_pr_review.rs

Documentation:
- add-pr-comment: clarify start_line must be strictly less than line
- reply-to-pr-comment: explain parentCommentId=1 targets root comment
- upload-attachment: document ##vso[] scan UTF-8 boundary behavior
- mcp.rs: log warning on report-incomplete write failure

Co-authored-by: jamesadevine <4742697+jamesadevine@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: flush NDJSON file after write to prevent stale reads

tokio::fs::File::write_all may buffer data internally without
flushing to the filesystem. Under load (e.g., full test suite),
a subsequent read_to_string could see stale data, causing
write_safe_output_file_with_maximum to miss already-written
entries and exceed the configured limit.

Adding an explicit flush() ensures data reaches the filesystem
before the function returns.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address 3 critical security issues from review

1. Self-approval guard for submit-pr-review and update-pr vote:
   Both voting paths now fetch the PR createdBy.id and block positive
   votes (approve, approve-with-suggestions) when the authenticated
   identity is the PR author.

2. ##vso[ pipeline command neutralization in core sanitize():
   Added neutralize_pipeline_commands() step that wraps ##vso[ and ##[
   sequences in backticks, preventing ADO from interpreting them as
   logging commands. This provides defense-in-depth for all text fields
   across all safe output tools.

3. resolve-pr-review-thread fail-closed allowed-statuses:
   Changed from permissive default (empty = all allowed) to fail-closed
   (empty = all rejected). Added compile-time validator
   validate_resolve_pr_thread_statuses() that requires explicit
   allowed-statuses config, matching the pattern of submit-pr-review
   allowed-events and update-pr allowed-votes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address 7 high-severity issues from review

4. link-work-items: Added required target config field (reuses
   CommentTarget enum from comment-on-work-item). Execution now
   fails if target is not configured, preventing unrestricted
   cross-project work item linking.

5. queue-build: Added ADO variable injection check for template
   parameter values. Rejects values containing \$(, \${{ or \$[
   syntax that could reference pipeline variables.

6. upload-attachment: Fixed path traversal via backslash by
   splitting on both / and \\ in .., .git, and absolute path
   checks. Added tests for backslash-based traversal.

7. create-git-tag + create-branch: Added comprehensive git ref
   name validation via shared validate_git_ref_name() helper.
   Rejects .., @{, ~, ^, :, ?, *, [, \\, //, .lock suffix,
   leading dots in path components, and trailing dots.

8. add-build-tag: Added allow-any-build config (default: false).
   When not explicitly enabled, only the current pipeline build
   (BUILD_BUILDID) can be tagged.

9. update-pr: Changed auto-complete to use the agent's own
   identity from _apis/connectiondata instead of the PR
   createdBy.id, providing correct audit attribution.

10. report-incomplete: Added sanitize_fields() call before using
    the reason field in execute.rs to prevent injection via
    unsanitized agent output.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: component-wise .. check in add_pr_comment, remove spurious pending status, use resolve_repo_name helper

fix: improve PDF extraction for WZ/delivery-note documents

Root cause: isPdfTextUsable() required invoice-specific keywords (faktura,
nip, netto, vat) — WZ (wydanie zewnetrzne) documents from ERP systems have
none of these, so valid extracted text was rejected and the AI received nothing.

Changes:
- parse-invoice.ts: isPdfTextUsable() - added WZ/business-document keywords:
  wydanie, dostaw, odbiorca, magazyn, ilosc, wartosc, zamowi, zleceni, koszt, uslug
- parse-invoice.ts: parseTextWithRegex() - added WZ_KEYWORDS pattern to detect
  'wydanie zewnetrzne', 'WZ nr', 'dokument WZ' and classify as document_type:'other'
- parse-invoice-ai.ts: INSTRUCTIONS - explicitly mention WZ/delivery notes as
  document_type 'other', with instruction to still extract ALL fields (vendor,
  buyer, items, amounts, dates); added WZ buyer/seller context keywords
- ExpensesPage.tsx: PDF_KEYWORDS - added WZ/business keywords to client-side
  fast-path (wydanie, wz/, dostaw, odbiorca, ilosc, wartosc, magazyn)
- useParseInvoice.ts: INVOICE_KEYWORDS regex - added wydanie, dostawca,
  odbiorca, wz nr, wartosc to isNonDocumentImage detection

strategy to horizontal flip.

Build conv_cifar_v5 suite (12 configs) exploring five directions:

  to capture higher-order patterns from first-stage feature maps

  whether the 12% train-test gap can be reduced at higher capacity

commit.message:
Extend arena copy-out to List, Closure, and ADT types (#72)

* Extend copy-out to ADT with copy-type fields; fix LowerPrint Unit type mismatch

- CanCopyOutArena now handles TNamedType (ADTs) where all constructors
  have the same arity and all fields are copy types (Int, Float, Bool).
  Type parameters are substituted with concrete type arguments.
- Fix LowerPrint to return a proper Unit value via LowerUnitValue()
  instead of returning the input temp with a Unit type (type/value
  mismatch that caused SEGFAULT when ADT copy-out was triggered).
- List and closure copy-out deferred: shallow copy is unsafe because
  internal pointers (tail, env) may reference freed arena memory.
- Add 10 new unit tests for ADT copy-out (positive and negative cases).
- Update 3 snapshot tests for the LowerPrint IR change.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/MattiasHognas/Ashes/sessions/2868f58f-de74-4892-896d-a918e55a4a88

Co-authored-by: MattiasHognas <1923344+MattiasHognas@users.noreply.github.com>

* plan: deep copy-out for List and Closure

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/MattiasHognas/Ashes/sessions/df16e223-0531-43bd-869e-6ecbd50d1263

Co-authored-by: MattiasHognas <1923344+MattiasHognas@users.noreply.github.com>

* Extend copy-out to List (deep CopyOutList) and Closure (CopyOutClosure + 24-byte layout)

- Add CopyOutList IR instruction: deep cons-chain copy at runtime. Walks
  tail pointers, allocating and relinking each cell. Safe for TList with
  copy-type (Int/Float/Bool) or TStr elements.
- Add CopyOutClosure IR instruction: copies the 24-byte closure struct
  and its env block. Reads env_size from closure[16] to know how many
  bytes to copy for the env.
- Change closure layout from 16 to 24 bytes: {code, env, env_size}.
  MakeClosure IR instruction now carries EnvSizeBytes. EmitMakeClosure
  stores env_size at offset 16. CallClosure unchanged (reads only
  offsets 0 and 8).
- Add CopyOutKind enum and GetCopyOutKind() to replace CanCopyOutArena
  for the general case. PopOwnershipScope and LowerCall Phase 3 now
  dispatch to the correct copy-out instruction based on kind.
- Add IsCopyOutSafeElement() for list element type check.
- Update Binding.Self to carry EnvSizeBytes for self-referential closure
  reconstruction.
- 7 new unit tests, 5 updated tests. All 1020 tests pass.

commit.message:
feat: implement Mapper 208 (MMC3 with protection LUT and fixed 32KB PRG) (#1978)

* feat: implement Mapper 208 (MMC3 with protection LUT and fixed 32KB PRG)

Adds Mapper208 for the MMC3 clone board with copy-protection mechanism
(iNES mapper 208).

Hardware behaviour:
- MMC3 variant; PRG always maps a fixed 32KB block (four consecutive
  8KB banks) selected by exRegs[5]; CHR/IRQ/mirroring are standard MMC3
- Power-on: exRegs[5] = 3 (banks 12-15 at $8000-$FFFF)
- $4800-$4FFF / $6800-$6FFF write: PRG block = (val & 1) | ((val>>3) & 2)
  (bits 0 and 4 of value select block 0-3)
- $5000-$57FF write: LUT index register (exRegs[4])
- $5800-$5FFF write: shadow reg exRegs[addr&3] = value XOR LUT[exRegs[4]]
- $5800-$5FFF read: returns exRegs[addr&3]
- 256-byte protection LUT embedded as constant

14 unit tests covering registration, power-on state, PRG block
selection, LUT/shadow register mechanism, CHR, IRQ, and snapshot.

Closes #1170

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address review comments for mapper 208

- Delegate wram_size/wram_snapshot/load_wram_snapshot to inner MMC3
  so PRG-RAM is properly persisted in save states and battery saves.
- Fix restore_registers to handle legacy MMC3-only snapshots: gate on
  expected length and reset ex_regs to power-on defaults (PRG block=3)
  when the snapshot is shorter than expected.
- Delegate capabilities() to inner MMC3 for consistent max_prg_ram_kb.
- Fix confusing test comment in prg_block_write_4800_extracts_bits_0_and_4.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: implement Mapper 197 (MMC3 clone with 4KB/2KB CHR granularity) (#1977)

* feat: implement Mapper 197 (MMC3 clone with 4KB/2KB CHR granularity)

Adds Mapper197 for the MMC3 clone board used in various pirate titles
(iNES mapper 197).

Hardware behaviour:
- Identical to MMC3 except for CHR bank mapping:
  - Mode 0 (bit 7 = 0): reg[0] selects 4KB at $0000-$0FFF, reg[2]
    selects 2KB at $1000-$17FF, reg[3] selects 2KB at $1800-$1FFF
  - Mode 1 (bit 7 = 1): reg[2] selects 4KB at $0000-$0FFF, reg[0]
    selects 2KB at both $1000-$17FF and $1800-$1FFF
- PRG mapping, IRQ, and mirroring are standard MMC3

10 unit tests covering registration, CHR mode 0 and mode 1 bank
selection, PRG power-on state, IRQ, and snapshot round-trip.

Closes #1149

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address review comments for mapper 197

- Use MMC3Mapper::new(ctx) instead of new_with_irq_mode so PRG-RAM
  presence/size and IRQ variant follow the ROM header/metadata.
- Delegate wram_size/wram_snapshot/load_wram_snapshot to inner MMC3
  for correct save-state and battery-save persistence.
- Delegate capabilities() to inner MMC3 for consistent max_prg_ram_kb.
- Add IRQ delegation test to verify MMC3 IRQ clocking works through
  the mapper wrapper.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: prevent orchestrate poller from reactivating issues with already-merged PRs (#706)

Two compounding bugs caused false stall detection on completed issues:

1. Terminal statuses (merged/closed) from WAVE_STATUS were never
   persisted to the state file, so detect_stalls always saw "pending"
   for completed issues on future poll cycles.

2. No closed-issue guard in recover_stalled_issue() meant recovery
   comments (/reclarify) were posted on closed issues when label
   fetches failed and determine_phase() returned "no_labels".

Fix 1: After logging wave statuses, persist merged/closed statuses
back to the state file so detect_stalls can skip them.

Fix 2: Check GitHub issue state at the top of recover_stalled_issue()
and skip recovery if the issue is closed (defence-in-depth).

commit.message:
﻿CADExchange: 修复 Revolve 轴在引用草图段时仅写 RefLocalID，缺少独立 ReferenceEntity 节点，导致引用结构与 Edge/Face 不统一，跨端解析时无法稳定还原父草图与段信息。

【问题详情与根因】
Revolve 轴在引用草图段时仅写 RefLocalID，缺少独立 ReferenceEntity 节点，导致引用结构与 Edge/Face 不统一，跨端解析时无法稳定还原父草图与段信息。

【修复方案与实现】
在 TinyXMLSerializer 的 Revolve Axis 序列化路径中，当存在 RefLocalID 且未提供 referenceEntity 时自动补写 Type=SketchSeg 的 ReferenceEntity（ParentFeatureID 与 SegmentLocalID）；在反序列化路径中识别 SketchSeg 并回填 RefLocalID，保持旧写侧行为兼容。同时在 MigrationRegressionTest 新增回归用例，覆盖保存与读取链路，确保后续重构不会回退此语义。`n`nCo-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

  includes staleSessionsFiltered and filteredStaleRoles.

feat: implement Mapper 196 (MMC3 with address scrambling and 32KB PRG override) (#1976)

* feat: implement Mapper 196 (MMC3 with address scrambling and 32KB PRG override)

Adds Mapper196 for the MMC3 clone board used in various pirate titles
(iNES mapper 196).

Hardware behaviour:
- MMC3 variant with two differences from standard MMC3:
  1. Address bit scrambling on register writes:
     - $8000–$BFFF: new A0 = A1|A2|A3
     - $C000–$FFFF: new A0 = A2|A3
  2. 32KiB PRG override: $6000–$6FFF write sets a block index
     (= (val & 0xF) | (val >> 4)) and maps four consecutive 8KB banks
     across $8000–$FFFF. Once activated, persists until power cycle.
- CHR, IRQ, and mirroring behave as standard MMC3.

20 unit tests covering registration, address scrambling, PRG override,
power-on state, CHR banking, snapshot round-trip, and IRQ.

Closes #1147

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address review comments for mapper 196

- Delegate wram_size/wram_snapshot/load_wram_snapshot to inner MMC3
  so PRG-RAM is properly persisted in save states and battery saves.
- Add hard-reset handling via initialize_ram/reset pattern (mapper233
  style): prg_locked and prg_block are cleared only on power cycle,
  not on soft reset.
- Delegate capabilities() to inner MMC3 for consistent max_prg_ram_kb.
- Update module docs to clarify that reads at $6000-$7FFF always
  access PRG-RAM, while writes at $6000-$6FFF are intercepted for
  the PRG override latch.
